# Chat Module — API Documentation

This document is the complete, self-contained reference for the Elay Customer Chat
module backend. It is written for a client developer (Flutter) who has no access to
the backend source code. Every endpoint, field, validation rule, error, and real-time
event described here has been verified directly against the running implementation.

Where the actual backend behavior differs from what a product specification might
assume, this document follows the code and calls out the discrepancy explicitly with
a footnote: *"Note: differs from originally assumed spec — verified against actual
implementation on Jul 4, 2026."*

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Overview](#1-overview)
2. [Authentication](#2-authentication)
3. [Standard Response & Error Format](#3-standard-response--error-format)
4. [DateTime, Localization & Data Caveats](#4-datetime-localization--data-caveats)
5. [File URLs & Static Assets](#5-file-urls--static-assets)
6. [Rate Limiting](#6-rate-limiting)
7. [Pagination](#7-pagination)
8. [Endpoint Reference](#8-endpoint-reference)
   - [8.1 Profile](#81-profile)
   - [8.2 Users & Discovery](#82-users--discovery)
   - [8.3 Conversations](#83-conversations)
   - [8.4 Messages](#84-messages)
   - [8.5 Files](#85-files)
   - [8.6 Group Management](#86-group-management)
   - [8.7 Channel Management](#87-channel-management)
   - [8.8 Blocking & Muting](#88-blocking--muting)
9. [Real-Time Communication (SignalR)](#9-real-time-communication-signalr)
10. [Known Backend Limitations](#10-known-backend-limitations)

---

## 1. Overview

The Chat module lets Customer-app users exchange messages in three conversation
types:

| Type | Enum value | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `PV` | `1` | One-to-one private conversation between exactly two users. |
| `Group` | `2` | Multi-member conversation. Any member can send messages. Has admins and regular members. |
| `Channel` | `3` | Multi-member conversation with a unique public `Handle`. Only admins can send messages; regular members are read-only. |

The conversation `Type` is serialized as its **string name** (`"PV"`, `"Group"`,
`"Channel"`), not its numeric value — see [Section 8.3](#83-conversations).

The module has two transport layers that a client **must both** integrate:

- **REST API** (base path `/chat/...`) for all CRUD-style operations: fetching lists,
  sending/editing/deleting messages, managing members, blocking, etc.
- **SignalR WebSocket hub** (`/hubs/chat`) for real-time push events: new messages,
  edits, deletes, read receipts, unread counters, online presence, and system
  messages (e.g. "user joined the group").

A message sent via REST is **not** delivered to other clients through the HTTP
response alone — the sender's own client receives the created message as the HTTP
response body, but **every** participant (including the sender, in some setups)
receives the message via the `ReceiveMessage` SignalR event. The frontend must not
rely on REST responses alone for real-time updates to conversations it is not
actively posting to.

---

## 2. Authentication

### 2.1 Login

Chat endpoints are not self-contained for authentication — they reuse the app-wide
JWT login flow.

```
POST /Auth/Login
Content-Type: application/json
```

```json
{
  "username": "sara.ahmadi",
  "password": "P@ssw0rd123",
  "deviceInfo": {
    "deviceID": "BE2A.250530.026.F3",
    "deviceName": "sdk_gphone64_x86_64",
    "deviceVersion": "16",
    "appVersion": "2.113.0",
    "platform": "android"
  }
}
```

A successful login (`HTTP 200`) returns an object containing (at minimum) an access
token and a refresh token. Store both securely (see Phase 1 of the implementation
plan).

### 2.2 Using the token

Every Chat REST endpoint requires:

```
Authorization: Bearer {accessToken}
```

- **Access token lifetime**: 1440 minutes (24 hours).
- **Refresh token lifetime**: 7 days.
- To renew an expired access token:

```
POST /Auth/RefreshToken
Content-Type: application/json
```

There is no chat-specific token refresh; use the app-wide refresh flow and re-apply
the new access token to both the REST client and the SignalR connection (SignalR
does not automatically pick up a renewed token on an already-open connection — see
[Section 9.2](#92-connecting)).

### 2.3 User identity

The backend derives the calling user's id from the JWT `NameIdentifier` claim on
every request (REST and SignalR alike). There is no separate "chat user id" —
it is the same numeric `AppUser.Id` used across the whole application.

### 2.4 SignalR authentication

SignalR cannot send an `Authorization` header on the initial WebSocket handshake in
most Flutter client configurations, so the token must be passed as a query string
parameter:

```
wss://{host}/hubs/chat?access_token={accessToken}
```

The backend explicitly special-cases this: `OnMessageReceived` reads
`access_token` from the query string **only** for requests whose path starts with
`/hubs/chat`. Do not rely on this behavior for any other endpoint.

---

## 3. Standard Response & Error Format

> **Note: differs from originally assumed spec** — the API does **not** wrap
> responses in a custom `{ isSuccess, value, error }` envelope. It uses plain HTTP
> status codes with the raw DTO as the body on success, and an RFC 7807
> `ProblemDetails` object on failure. Verified against actual implementation on
> Jul 4, 2026.

### 3.1 Success

- **200 OK** is used for **every** successful operation, including resource
  creation (e.g. creating a group or channel). The API never returns `201 Created`.
- The response body is the raw DTO (see each endpoint below), or empty for
  operations that return no data (e.g. `DELETE /chat/messages/{id}`).
- JSON property names are **camelCase** (`conversationId`, `senderName`, etc.) —
  this is the ASP.NET Core Minimal API default and is applied uniformly.
- Enum properties that are **not** explicitly mapped to a string in a DTO (there are
  none of these in the Chat DTOs — all enums are converted with `.ToString()`
  before being placed in a DTO) — every conversation `Type` and member `Role` you
  receive is already a human-readable string, never a raw number.

### 3.2 Failure

All failures — including validation errors, business-rule violations, and
unhandled exceptions — return a `ProblemDetails` object:

```json
{
  "type": "https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1",
  "title": "Bad Request",
  "status": 400,
  "detail": "شما عضو این مکالمه نیستید",
  "traceId": "00-4b6a1e3e2f8b4c6c9e2a1e3e2f8b4c6c-8b4c6c9e2a1e3e2f-00"
}
```

- **`detail`** is the field the client should show to the user (it is already in
  Persian).
- Almost all Chat errors are returned with **HTTP 400**, regardless of their
  semantic error type (see 3.3). The only Chat endpoint that returns a different
  status for a business error is channel creation, which returns **HTTP 409** for a
  duplicate handle.
- Multiple validation failures on the same request are concatenated into a single
  `detail` string, separated by newlines (`\r\n`), e.g.:
  `"نام گروه الزامی است\r\nحداقل یک عضو باید انتخاب شود\r\n"`.

### 3.3 Internal error classification (informational only)

Internally every error carries a `Code`, a Persian `Description`, and an
`ErrorType` (numeric enum: `Failure=0`, `NotFound=1`, `Validation=2`,
`Conflict=3`, `UnauthorizedAccess=4`, `AccessForbidden=5`). **This classification is
not exposed in the HTTP response** — the client only ever sees the `detail` string
and the HTTP status code (400 or 409). It is documented here only so you understand
why, for example, a FluentValidation failure and a "not a participant" failure look
identical on the wire (both are HTTP 400 with a `detail` string) even though they
are different error categories internally.

### 3.4 Complete error catalogue

| Error code (internal) | Persian message (`detail`) | HTTP status | When it occurs |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Chat.ConversationNotFound` | مکالمه یافت نشد | 400 | Conversation id doesn't exist, or PV target user doesn't exist/is inactive. |
| `Chat.NotAParticipant` | شما عضو این مکالمه نیستید | 400 | Caller is not an active member of the conversation (or already left it). |
| `Chat.CannotChatWithYourself` | امکان گفتگو با خودتان وجود ندارد | 400 | `targetUserId` equals the caller's own id (PV creation or block). |
| `Chat.HandleAlreadyTaken` | این شناسه کانال قبلاً ثبت شده است | **409** | Channel `handle` already used by another channel. |
| `Chat.NotGroupAdmin` | فقط مدیر گروه مجاز به این عملیات است | 400 | Non-admin attempts group-admin-only action, or conversation is not actually a `Group`. |
| `Chat.NotChannelAdmin` | فقط مدیر کانال مجاز به این عملیات است | 400 | Non-admin attempts channel-admin-only action, or conversation is not actually a `Channel`. |
| `Chat.MessageNotFound` | پیام یافت نشد | 400 | Message id doesn't exist (edit/delete/forward/mark-as-read). |
| `Chat.CannotEditMessage` | فقط فرستنده می‌تواند پیام را ویرایش کند | 400 | Caller is not the original sender. |
| `Chat.CannotDeleteMessage` | دسترسی برای حذف این پیام وجود ندارد | 400 | Caller is neither the sender nor a conversation admin. |
| `Chat.MessageIsDeleted` | پیام قبلاً حذف شده است | 400 | Edit/delete attempted on an already soft-deleted message. |
| `Chat.EmptyMessage` | پیام نمی‌تواند خالی باشد | 400 | `UploadChatFile` called with zero files. |
| `Chat.FileTooLarge` | حجم فایل بیش از ۵۰ مگابایت مجاز نیست | 400 | Any attachment exceeds 52,428,800 bytes (50 MB). |
| `Chat.ChannelMembersCannotSend` | اعضای کانال اجازه ارسال پیام ندارند | 400 | A non-admin channel member tries to send a message. |
| `Chat.AlreadyBlocked` | این کاربر قبلاً بلاک شده است | 400 | Block requested for a user already blocked by the caller. |
| `Chat.NotBlocked` | این کاربر بلاک نشده است | 400 | Unblock requested for a user that isn't currently blocked. |
| `Chat.UserBlocked` | امکان ارسال پیام به این کاربر وجود ندارد | 400 | Sending a PV message where a mutual block exists (either direction). |
| *(empty code)* | *(concatenated FluentValidation messages)* | 400 | Any request-shape validation failure (missing/invalid fields — see per-endpoint tables below). |
| *(unhandled exception)* | Persian generic message per endpoint, e.g. `خطا در ارسال پیام` | 400 | Any unexpected server exception. Logged server-side via Serilog; the client only sees the generic message. |

## 4. DateTime, Localization & Data Caveats

> ⚠️ **Critical — read before implementing any timestamp logic.**
> **Note: differs from originally assumed spec** — timestamps in this module are
> **not** uniformly UTC. Verified against actual implementation on Jul 4, 2026.

| Field | Source | Actual value |
|---|---|---|
| `ChatMessageDto.sentAt` | `ChatMessage.CreateDate`, populated by the audit interceptor via `SELECT GETDATE()` on SQL Server | **Database server's local time**, not UTC and not necessarily the API server's local time either. |
| `ChatMessageDto.editedAt` | Set explicitly in code as `DateTime.UtcNow` | **UTC**. |
| `ForwardedFromDto.originalSentAt` | Copied from the original message's `CreateDate` (same source as `sentAt`) | **Database server's local time.** |
| Conversation `joinedAt` / `leftAt` / block `blockedAt` (not exposed in any DTO today, informational only) | `DateTime.UtcNow` | **UTC.** |

**Practical guidance for the frontend:**

- Treat `sentAt` and `originalSentAt` as **naive local timestamps in the database
  server's timezone** (this backend's database and application servers are
  configured for Iran Standard Time, UTC+3:30, at the time of writing). Do **not**
  parse them as UTC and convert — that would shift displayed times by the server's
  UTC offset.
- Treat `editedAt` as **UTC** and convert to the device's local timezone for
  display.
- This inconsistency (`sentAt` = server-local, `editedAt` = UTC) is a backend bug
  candidate, not an intentional design. It is flagged again in
  [Section 10](#10-known-backend-limitations). Do not "fix" it on the client by
  guessing — ask the backend team to confirm the server's timezone offset before
  shipping any date-math logic (e.g. "sent 5 minutes ago").
- All Persian text (error messages, system messages) is server-generated and
  right-to-left. The client is responsible for RTL layout; the backend does no
  bidi processing.

## 5. File URLs & Static Assets

> **Note: differs from originally assumed spec** — there are **two different**
> static file base paths depending on what the file is for. Using the wrong one
> will silently 404. Verified against actual implementation on Jul 4, 2026.

Upload endpoints and profile/message DTOs return only a **relative filename**
(e.g. `"a1b2c3d4.jpg"`), never a full URL. The client must build the full URL
itself using one of two prefixes:

| Content type | DTO field(s) | URL pattern | Example |
|---|---|---|---|
| User avatars, group avatars, channel avatars | `ChatUserDto.avatarUrl`, `ChatMemberDto.avatarUrl`, `ChatProfileDto.avatarUrl`, `ConversationListItemDto.avatarUrl`, `ConversationDetailDto.avatarUrl`, `ChatMessageDto.senderAvatarUrl` | `{baseUrl}/images/{filename}` | `https://api.elay.example.com/images/a1b2c3d4.jpg` |
| Chat message attachments | `ChatFileDto.fileUrl` (used inside `ChatMessageDto.attachments`) | `{baseUrl}/chat_files/{filename}` | `https://api.elay.example.com/chat_files/f9e8d7c6.pdf` |

`{baseUrl}` is the same host/port you use for all other REST calls (i.e. the
Customer API base URL), **without** any `/chat` prefix.

- If `avatarUrl` is `null` or empty, render a default/placeholder avatar — do not
  attempt to build a URL from an empty string.
- Filenames returned by `POST /chat/files` (upload) are meant to be sent back to
  the server as-is in a subsequent `avatarUrl` field (e.g.
  `PUT /chat/groups/{id}` body) — do **not** prefix them with `/chat_files/` when
  sending them back to the server; only prefix them when building a URL to
  *display* an image.

## 6. Rate Limiting

Every Chat endpoint (REST) enforces a **fixed-window** rate limit policy shared
across the whole Customer API (not chat-specific):

| Setting | Value |
|---|---|
| Permit limit | 10 requests |
| Window | 20 seconds |
| Queue processing order | Oldest first |
| Queue limit | 3 (requests beyond the permit limit queue instead of failing immediately, up to 3 queued) |

When the limit and queue are both exhausted, the server returns **HTTP 429 Too
Many Requests**. The client must implement backoff/retry (see Phase 10 of the
implementation plan) — this is especially relevant for chat, where rapid actions
(fast typing + send, fast scroll-triggered pagination) can realistically hit 10
requests / 20 seconds.

SignalR method invocations (`JoinConversation`, `LeaveConversation`) are **not**
subject to this REST rate limiter.

---

## 7. Pagination

Only one endpoint is paginated: `GET /chat/conversations/{conversationId}/messages`.
It uses **cursor-based pagination**, not page-index pagination (unlike most of the
rest of the Elay backend).

| Parameter | Type | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `beforeMessageId` | `int?` (query) | `null` (first page = most recent messages) | Pass the smallest (`oldestMessageId`) message id from the previous page to load older messages. |
| `pageSize` | `int` (query) | `20` | Clamped server-side to the range `[1, 50]`. Any value `<= 0` sent by the client is also normalized to `20` at the endpoint layer before validation. |
| `search` | `string?` (query) | `null` | Free-text search within this conversation's non-deleted message text (case-insensitive substring match). When set, only matching messages are returned — pagination still applies. |

Response shape (`ChatMessagePageDto`):

```json
{
  "messages": [
    { "id": 1041, "conversationId": 55, "senderId": 12, "senderName": "سارا احمدی", "senderAvatarUrl": "images/sara.jpg", "text": "سلام، جلسه فردا ساعت چند است؟", "isDeleted": false, "isEdited": false, "sentAt": "2026-07-04T10:15:32", "editedAt": null, "replyTo": null, "forwardedFrom": null, "attachments": [] },
    { "id": 1042, "conversationId": 55, "senderId": 7, "senderName": "علی رضایی", "senderAvatarUrl": null, "text": "ساعت ۹ صبح", "isDeleted": false, "isEdited": false, "sentAt": "2026-07-04T10:16:05", "editedAt": null, "replyTo": { "messageId": 1041, "senderName": "سارا احمدی", "textPreview": "سلام، جلسه فردا ساعت چند است؟", "isDeleted": false }, "forwardedFrom": null, "attachments": [] }
  ],
  "hasMore": true,
  "oldestMessageId": 1041
}
```

- `messages` is returned in **ascending** id order (oldest → newest) within the
  page, ready to append directly above existing messages in a chat list that
  renders top-to-bottom-oldest-to-newest, or to reverse for a bottom-up list.
- `hasMore: true` means older messages exist beyond this page; pass
  `oldestMessageId` as the next `beforeMessageId` to continue.
- `oldestMessageId` is `null` only when `messages` is empty.
- There is no `afterMessageId` / forward-pagination parameter — the backend has no
  concept of "load newer messages via REST"; new messages only arrive via the
  `ReceiveMessage` SignalR event.

## 8. Endpoint Reference

All routes below are relative to the Customer API base URL. All require
`Authorization: Bearer {accessToken}` and are subject to the rate limit in
[Section 6](#6-rate-limiting) unless stated otherwise. All are `RequireAuthorization()`
protected — there are no anonymous Chat endpoints.

### 8.1 Profile

#### `GET /chat/profile`

Returns the current user's chat profile.

**Response `200` (`ChatProfileDto`):**

```json
{
  "userId": 12,
  "displayName": "سارا احمدی",
  "avatarUrl": "sara_profile.jpg",
  "phoneNumber": "09121234567"
}
```

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `userId` | int | |
| `displayName` | string | |
| `avatarUrl` | string? | Relative filename — prefix with `/images/` (Section 5). |
| `phoneNumber` | string | **Read-only in this screen** — see limitations. |

#### `PUT /chat/profile`

Updates the current user's display name and/or avatar. **Request is
`multipart/form-data`**, not JSON.

| Form field | Type | Required |
|---|---|---|
| `displayName` | string | No (send only the field(s) you're changing) |
| `avatar` | file | No |

At least one of `displayName` or `avatar` must be present, or the request fails
validation.

**Validation:**

| Rule | Message |
|---|---|
| `displayName` max length 100 (only checked when provided) | نام نباید بیشتر از ۱۰۰ کاراکتر باشد |
| At least one of `displayName`/`avatar` required | حداقل یک فیلد برای ویرایش الزامی است |

**Response:** `200 OK`, empty body.

> ⚠️ **Critical shared-state warning** (not in the original spec — verified against
> actual implementation on Jul 4, 2026): this endpoint does **not** write to a
> chat-specific profile table. It directly updates the shared `AppUser.Name` and
> `AppUser.ProfileImage` fields — the **same** name/avatar fields used
> everywhere else in the Elay Customer app (not just chat). Changing your "chat
> display name" here changes your display name application-wide. See
> [Section 10](#10-known-backend-limitations).

---

### 8.2 Users & Discovery

#### `GET /chat/users?search={text}`

Returns active users (excluding the caller) available to start a chat with.

| Query param | Type | Required |
|---|---|---|
| `search` | string? | No — filters by name or username, case-insensitive substring match. |

**Response `200` (`List<ChatUserDto>`):**

```json
[
  { "id": 7, "displayName": "علی رضایی", "avatarUrl": "ali.jpg" },
  { "id": 19, "displayName": "مریم کریمی", "avatarUrl": null }
]
```

This list is used both for starting a new PV conversation and for member pickers
when creating/editing groups and channels. It does **not** indicate which users are
already blocked by the caller, nor which are online — the client must combine this
with block-state (client-tracked) and `UserStatusChanged` events (Section 9) for
that information.

---

### 8.3 Conversations

#### `GET /chat/conversations?search={text}`

Returns the caller's active (not-left) conversations, most-recently-active first.

| Query param | Type | Required |
|---|---|---|
| `search` | string? | No — matches conversation name OR any message text within it. |

**Response `200` (`List<ConversationListItemDto>`):**

```json
[
  {
    "id": 55,
    "type": "PV",
    "name": "علی رضایی",
    "avatarUrl": "ali.jpg",
    "lastMessage": "ساعت ۹ صبح",
    "lastMessageAt": "2026-07-04T10:16:05",
    "unreadCount": 2,
    "isMuted": false
  },
  {
    "id": 61,
    "type": "Group",
    "name": "تیم فروش",
    "avatarUrl": null,
    "lastMessage": "فایل ارسال شد",
    "lastMessageAt": "2026-07-03T18:40:11",
    "unreadCount": 0,
    "isMuted": true
  }
]
```

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `type` | string | One of `"PV"`, `"Group"`, `"Channel"`. |
| `name` | string | For `PV`, this is currently stored as an **empty string** at creation time — see [Section 10](#10-known-backend-limitations) for the required client-side workaround (display the other participant's name instead). |
| `lastMessage` | string? | Raw text of the newest message, or `null` if the conversation has no messages yet or the newest message was a deleted/attachment-only message (text is nulled on delete). |
| `lastMessageAt` | string? (ISO datetime) | Server-local time — see [Section 4](#4-datetime-localization--data-caveats). `null` if no messages exist. |
| `unreadCount` | int | Count of messages from **other** senders with `id >` the caller's last-read message id. |
| `isMuted` | bool | Caller's own mute state for this conversation. |

Ordering is `lastMessageAt` descending, with conversations that have no messages
sorted last.

Sorting/reordering the list in real time as new messages arrive is a **client**
responsibility — this GET endpoint is not pushed to automatically; combine it with
the `ReceiveMessage` and `ConversationUpdated` SignalR events (Section 9).

#### `GET /chat/conversations/{conversationId}`

Returns full detail for one conversation, including its member list. Caller must
be an active participant.

**Response `200` (`ConversationDetailDto`):**

```json
{
  "id": 61,
  "type": "Group",
  "name": "تیم فروش",
  "avatarUrl": null,
  "description": null,
  "handle": null,
  "members": [
    { "userId": 12, "displayName": "سارا احمدی", "avatarUrl": "sara.jpg", "role": "Admin" },
    { "userId": 7, "displayName": "علی رضایی", "avatarUrl": "ali.jpg", "role": "Member" }
  ],
  "currentUserRole": "Admin",
  "isMuted": false
}
```

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `description` | string? | Only meaningful for `Channel`; always `null` for `PV`/`Group`. |
| `handle` | string? | Only set for `Channel`. |
| `members` | array | Only currently-active members (users who left are excluded). |
| `role` (per member) | string | `"Member"` or `"Admin"`. |
| `currentUserRole` | string | The caller's own role in this conversation. |

**Errors:** `Chat.NotAParticipant`, `Chat.ConversationNotFound`.

#### `POST /chat/conversations/pv/{targetUserId}`

Creates a new PV conversation with `targetUserId`, or returns the existing one if
it already exists (idempotent create-or-get).

**Response `200` (`ConversationDetailDto`)** — same shape as above, `type: "PV"`.

**Validation:** `targetUserId` (route) must resolve to `> 0`.

**Errors:**

| Error | Cause |
|---|---|
| `Chat.CannotChatWithYourself` | `targetUserId == callerId`. |
| `Chat.ConversationNotFound` | `targetUserId` doesn't exist or is inactive. |

> Note: creating/opening a PV conversation does **not** check block state in
> either direction. A blocked (or blocking) user's PV conversation can still be
> opened and its history read — only **sending** a message is blocked (see
> [Section 8.4](#84-messages)). This is intentional per the "read-only after
> block" behavior described in the product spec, and has been verified to match
> the code.

#### `POST /chat/groups` — Create group

**Request is `multipart/form-data`.**

| Form field | Type | Required |
|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | Yes |
| `avatar` | file | No |
| `memberUserIds` | string (comma-separated user ids, e.g. `"7,19,23"`) | No — repeat the field or comma-join; both are accepted since the server parses every value in the field and splits on commas. |

**Validation** (applied to the underlying command, so a validation failure surfaces
even though the endpoint itself does light parsing):

| Rule | Message |
|---|---|
| `name` required | نام گروه الزامی است |
| `name` max length 100 | نام گروه نباید بیشتر از ۱۰۰ کاراکتر باشد |
| `memberUserIds` must be non-empty | حداقل یک عضو باید انتخاب شود |

**Response `200` (`ConversationDetailDto`)**, `type: "Group"`, caller is
automatically added as `"Admin"`.

Invalid/inactive member ids in `memberUserIds` are **silently dropped** — the
group is still created with whatever valid members remain (this can result in a
group being created with **zero** additional members even if the client sent ids,
with no error surfaced). The client should re-fetch conversation detail after
creation to confirm the actual member list rather than trusting its own input.

#### `POST /chat/channels` — Create channel

**Request is `multipart/form-data`.**

| Form field | Type | Required |
|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | Yes |
| `handle` | string | Yes — must be globally unique among channels. |
| `description` | string | No |
| `avatar` | file | No |
| `memberUserIds` | string (comma-separated) | No |

**Validation:**

| Rule | Message |
|---|---|
| `name` required, max 100 | نام کانال الزامی است / نام کانال نباید بیشتر از ۱۰۰ کاراکتر باشد |
| `handle` required, max 50, pattern `^[a-z0-9_]+$` | شناسه کانال الزامی است / شناسه کانال نباید بیشتر از ۵۰ کاراکتر باشد / شناسه باید فقط شامل حروف کوچک انگلیسی، عدد و _ باشد |
| `description` max 500 (only if provided) | توضیحات نباید بیشتر از ۵۰۰ کاراکتر باشد |

**Response `200` (`ConversationDetailDto`)**, `type: "Channel"`, caller is
automatically added as `"Admin"`.

**Errors:**

| Error | HTTP status | Cause |
|---|---|---|
| `Chat.HandleAlreadyTaken` | **409** | `handle` already used by another channel. |

Same silent-drop behavior for invalid `memberUserIds` as group creation applies
here too.

#### `POST /chat/conversations/{conversationId}/leave`

Removes the caller from a `Group` or `Channel` (soft — sets a `leftAt` timestamp,
does not delete history). Broadcasts a `SystemMessage` event
(Section 9) announcing the departure.

**Response:** `200 OK`, empty body.

**Errors:** `Chat.NotAParticipant`.

> ⚠️ This endpoint has **no server-side restriction preventing it from being
> called on a `PV` conversation.** See [Section 10](#10-known-backend-limitations)
> for the resulting duplicate-conversation bug if a client allows "leave" on a PV
> chat. The frontend must not expose a "leave" action on PV chat screens.

#### `POST /chat/conversations/{conversationId}/mute`

Mutes or unmutes the conversation for the caller only.

```json
{ "isMuted": true }
```

**Response:** `200 OK`, empty body. **Errors:** `Chat.NotAParticipant`.

Muting suppresses in-app notification-center entries for new messages in this
conversation (see [Section 9.6](#96-notifications--mute-interaction)) but does
**not** suppress the real-time `ReceiveMessage` SignalR broadcast — a muted
conversation's messages still arrive live if the client is subscribed to its
SignalR group. The client is responsible for suppressing any notification banner
it would otherwise show, based on the conversation's `isMuted` flag.

#### `POST /chat/conversations/{conversationId}/read`

Marks all messages up to and including `lastReadMessageId` as read for the caller.

```json
{ "lastReadMessageId": 1042 }
```

**Validation:** both ids must be `> 0`.

**Errors:** `Chat.NotAParticipant`, `Chat.MessageNotFound` (if `lastReadMessageId`
doesn't belong to this conversation).

**Response:** `200 OK`, empty body. Side effects (see Section 9):

- If the conversation is `PV`, broadcasts `MessageRead` to the conversation's
  SignalR group.
- Always broadcasts `ConversationUpdated` with the caller's recomputed
  `unreadCount` for this conversation.
- If `lastReadMessageId` is not greater than the caller's currently stored
  last-read id, the call is a harmless no-op (`200 OK`, no events fired) —
  the read pointer never moves backward.

---

### 8.4 Messages

#### `GET /chat/conversations/{conversationId}/messages`

See [Section 7](#7-pagination) for full parameter and response documentation.

**Errors:** `Chat.NotAParticipant`.

#### `POST /chat/conversations/{conversationId}/messages` — Send message

**Request is `multipart/form-data`.**

| Form field | Type | Required |
|---|---|---|
| `text` | string | Conditionally — see validation. |
| `replyToMessageId` | int | No |
| `attachments` | file[] | Conditionally — see validation. |

**Validation:**

| Rule | Message |
|---|---|
| `conversationId` (route) `> 0` | شناسه مکالمه نامعتبر است |
| `text` max length 4000 | متن پیام نباید بیشتر از ۴۰۰۰ کاراکتر باشد |
| At least one of `text` or `attachments` required | پیام نمی‌تواند خالی باشد |
| Every attachment `<= 52,428,800` bytes (50 MB) | حجم فایل بیش از ۵۰ مگابایت مجاز نیست |

**Business-rule errors:**

| Error | Cause |
|---|---|
| `Chat.NotAParticipant` | Caller is not an active member. |
| `Chat.ChannelMembersCannotSend` | Conversation is a `Channel` and caller's role is `Member`, not `Admin`. |
| `Chat.UserBlocked` | Conversation is `PV` and a block exists in **either** direction between the two participants. **This check only applies to `PV` conversations** — Group and Channel messages are never blocked by a per-user block relationship. |

**Response `200` (`ChatMessageDto`):**

```json
{
  "id": 1043,
  "conversationId": 55,
  "senderId": 12,
  "senderName": "سارا احمدی",
  "senderAvatarUrl": "sara.jpg",
  "text": "باشه، می‌بینمت",
  "isDeleted": false,
  "isEdited": false,
  "sentAt": "2026-07-04T10:20:11",
  "editedAt": null,
  "replyTo": {
    "messageId": 1042,
    "senderName": "علی رضایی",
    "textPreview": "ساعت ۹ صبح",
    "isDeleted": false
  },
  "forwardedFrom": null,
  "attachments": [
    { "id": 88, "fileName": "map.png", "fileUrl": "d41d8cd98f00.png", "fileSize": 204800, "contentType": "image/png" }
  ]
}
```

If a `replyToMessageId` is supplied but does not refer to an existing message in
the **same** conversation, it is silently ignored (`replyTo` will be `null` in the
response, no error).

This same DTO shape is what arrives via the `ReceiveMessage` SignalR event to
**other** participants — the sender's own client should treat the HTTP response as
the authoritative "message sent" confirmation and may choose to ignore its own
echo if the SignalR event also arrives for the sender's own connection (the sender
is included in the conversation's SignalR group like any other member).

Sending a message also creates an in-app `NotificationCenter` entry for every
**other**, **non-muted**, currently-active participant (see
[Section 9.6](#96-notifications--mute-interaction)).

#### `PUT /chat/messages/{messageId}` — Edit message

```json
{ "text": "متن ویرایش‌شده پیام" }
```

**Validation:** `messageId` (route) `> 0`; `text` required (non-empty), max 4000.

**Errors:** `Chat.MessageNotFound`, `Chat.MessageIsDeleted`,
`Chat.CannotEditMessage` (caller is not the original sender — **there is no
admin-override for editing**, only for deleting).

**Response:** `200 OK`, empty body. Broadcasts `MessageEdited` (Section 9).

> Editing only ever changes `text`. There is no way to edit/add/remove attachments
> on an existing message via this endpoint.

#### `DELETE /chat/messages/{messageId}` — Delete message

**Validation:** `messageId` (route) `> 0`.

**Errors:** `Chat.MessageNotFound`, `Chat.MessageIsDeleted`,
`Chat.CannotDeleteMessage` (caller is neither the sender nor an admin of the
conversation).

**Response:** `200 OK`, empty body. Broadcasts `MessageDeleted` (Section 9).

This is a **soft delete**: the message row is kept (`isDeleted = true`,
`text` is set to `null` server-side). Any subsequent `GET messages` call for this
message will show `isDeleted: true, text: null`. **The backend does not supply a
placeholder string** (e.g. "this message was deleted") — the frontend must render
its own placeholder text when `isDeleted` is `true`. Attachments on a deleted
message are **not** removed from the response — `attachments` will still contain
the original files. Decide in the UI whether to still show/allow downloading
attachments on a deleted message (the backend does not hide them).

#### `POST /chat/messages/{messageId}/forward` — Forward message

```json
{ "targetConversationIds": [61, 74] }
```

**Validation:** `messageId` (route) `> 0`; `targetConversationIds` non-empty.

**Errors:** `Chat.MessageNotFound`, `Chat.MessageIsDeleted`,
`Chat.NotAParticipant` (caller is not a member of the **source** conversation of
the message being forwarded).

**Response:** `200 OK`, empty body (no DTO returned for the newly created
forwarded messages — the client relies on the `ReceiveMessage` SignalR event for
each successfully forwarded target, see below).

> ⚠️ Several non-obvious behaviors verified in code, not in the original spec:
>
> 1. **Attachments are not copied.** A forwarded message carries over only the
>    original **text**, never its attachments. Forwarding a photo/file message
>    produces a text-only (or, if the original had no text, an **empty**) message
>    in the target conversation, tagged with `forwardedFrom` metadata.
> 2. **Per-target failures are silent.** For each id in `targetConversationIds`,
>    the backend independently checks that the caller is a member of that target
>    conversation and (for `Channel` targets) that the caller is an admin there.
>    Any target that fails either check is **skipped with no error** — the overall
>    request still returns `200 OK` even if **every** target was skipped. The
>    client cannot tell from the HTTP response which targets actually received the
>    message. Rely on `ReceiveMessage` events (one per successful target) as the
>    only source of truth for what was actually forwarded, and consider a
>    short client-side timeout to show "message may not have been delivered to all
>    selected chats" if expected events don't arrive.
> 3. Forwarding does **not** create a `NotificationCenter` entry for recipients
>    (see [Section 10](#10-known-backend-limitations)) even though it does still
>    broadcast the real-time `ReceiveMessage` event.

---

### 8.5 Files

#### `POST /chat/files` — Upload standalone attachment(s)

**Request is `multipart/form-data`**, field name `files` (repeat for multiple
files).

**Validation:**

| Rule | Message |
|---|---|
| At least one file | پیام نمی‌تواند خالی باشد *(reuses the "empty message" error code)* |
| Every file `<= 52,428,800` bytes | حجم فایل بیش از ۵۰ مگابایت مجاز نیست |

**Response `200` (`List<ChatFileDto>`):**

```json
[
  { "id": 0, "fileName": "invoice.pdf", "fileUrl": "9f8e7d6c5b4a.pdf", "fileSize": 1048576, "contentType": "application/pdf" }
]
```

> Note the returned `id` is always `0` for this endpoint — files uploaded here are
> not yet attached to any message, so there is no attachment row id yet.

**When is this endpoint actually needed?** In most flows you do **not** need to
call this separately: `POST /chat/conversations/{id}/messages` (send message,
Section 8.4) already accepts `attachments` directly as part of the same
multipart request and performs its own independent 50 MB-per-file validation.
Use `POST /chat/files` only if your UX needs to upload/preview a file **before**
the user has decided to send it (e.g. a persistent "compose" attachment tray)
in a way decoupled from the send action — but note that files uploaded via this
endpoint are **not referenced anywhere afterward**; there is no
"attach this previously-uploaded file to my next message" parameter on
`SendChatMessage`. If your UX needs "attach now, send later," send the raw
`IFormFile` bytes to the send-message endpoint directly instead of pre-uploading.

---

### 8.6 Group Management

All endpoints in this section require the caller to be an **`Admin`** participant
of a conversation whose `Type` is actually `Group` (both checks are enforced
server-side; calling these on a `Channel` id, even as its admin, fails with
`Chat.NotGroupAdmin`).

#### `POST /chat/groups/{conversationId}/members` — Add members

```json
{ "userIds": [23, 41] }
```

**Validation:** `conversationId` `> 0`; `userIds` non-empty.

**Errors:** `Chat.NotAParticipant`, `Chat.NotGroupAdmin`.

**Response:** `200 OK`, empty body. Invalid/inactive/already-member ids are
silently filtered out (if **all** ids are filtered out, the call still succeeds
with `200 OK` and adds nobody). For each member actually added, a `SystemMessage`
event is broadcast: `"{name} به گروه اضافه شد"`.

#### `DELETE /chat/groups/{conversationId}/members/{targetUserId}` — Remove member

**Validation:** both ids `> 0`.

**Errors:** `Chat.NotAParticipant`, `Chat.NotGroupAdmin` (also returned if
`targetUserId` is currently an `Admin` — **admins cannot be removed by another
admin through this endpoint**, they must leave voluntarily via
`POST /chat/conversations/{id}/leave`).

**Response:** `200 OK`, empty body. Broadcasts a `SystemMessage`:
`"{name} از گروه خارج شد"`.

#### `PUT /chat/groups/{conversationId}` — Update group info

```json
{ "name": "تیم فروش و بازاریابی", "avatarUrl": "9f8e7d6c5b4a.jpg" }
```

Body is JSON (not multipart) — if you need to change the avatar, first call
`POST /chat/files` (Section 8.5) or reuse the filename returned from a prior
upload, then send that filename as `avatarUrl` here.

**Validation:**

| Rule | Message |
|---|---|
| `name` max 100 (only if provided) | نام گروه نباید بیشتر از ۱۰۰ کاراکتر باشد |
| At least one of `name`/`avatarUrl` required | حداقل یک فیلد برای ویرایش الزامی است |

**Errors:** `Chat.NotAParticipant`, `Chat.NotGroupAdmin`,
`Chat.ConversationNotFound`.

**Response:** `200 OK`, empty body. If `name` changed, broadcasts a
`SystemMessage`: `"نام گروه به {name} تغییر یافت"` (no system message is sent for
an avatar-only change).

---

### 8.7 Channel Management

Same shape as Group Management (Section 8.6), but the conversation must be a
`Channel`, the caller must be its `Admin`, and errors use `Chat.NotChannelAdmin`
instead of `Chat.NotGroupAdmin`.

#### `POST /chat/channels/{conversationId}/members` — Add members

Identical request/response/validation shape to the group version. System message:
`"{name} به کانال اضافه شد"`.

#### `DELETE /chat/channels/{conversationId}/members/{targetUserId}` — Remove member

Identical shape to the group version (admins cannot be removed). System message:
`"{name} از کانال خارج شد"`.

#### `PUT /chat/channels/{conversationId}` — Update channel info

```json
{ "name": "اطلاعیه‌های شرکت", "description": "کانال رسمی اطلاعیه‌ها", "avatarUrl": "c1d2e3f4.jpg" }
```

**Validation:**

| Rule | Message |
|---|---|
| `name` max 100 (only if provided) | نام کانال نباید بیشتر از ۱۰۰ کاراکتر باشد |
| `description` max 500 (only if provided) | توضیحات نباید بیشتر از ۵۰۰ کاراکتر باشد |
| At least one of `name`/`description`/`avatarUrl` required | حداقل یک فیلد برای ویرایش الزامی است |

**Note:** the channel's `handle` (its unique public identifier) **cannot be
changed** via this or any other endpoint — there is no `PATCH handle` operation
anywhere in the API.

**Response:** `200 OK`, empty body. If `name` changed, broadcasts:
`"نام کانال به {name} تغییر یافت"`.

---

### 8.8 Blocking & Muting

Mute is documented under [Section 8.3](#83-conversations) (it's a per-conversation
setting). Block is per-**user** and is global across all PV conversations with
that user.

#### `POST /chat/users/{targetUserId}/block`

**Validation:** none beyond the route constraint (`int`).

**Errors:**

| Error | Cause |
|---|---|
| `Chat.CannotChatWithYourself` | `targetUserId == callerId`. |
| `Chat.ConversationNotFound` | Target user doesn't exist or is inactive. *(Reused error code — despite the name, this means "user not found," not "conversation.")* |
| `Chat.AlreadyBlocked` | Already blocked by the caller. |

**Response:** `200 OK`, empty body. **This action is silent** — no notification or
system message is sent to the blocked user; they are not informed. Blocking is
one-directional in storage (a row with `blockerUserId`/`blockedUserId`) but the
`Chat.UserBlocked` send-message check tests **both** directions
(see [Section 8.4](#84-messages)), so a blocked user also cannot message the
blocker back.

**What blocking does *not* do:**

- Does not hide the PV conversation from either party's conversation list.
- Does not prevent either party from opening/reading the conversation's message
  history.
- Does not affect Group or Channel messaging between the same two users at all.
- Does not remove the block automatically if the other party blocks back, or ever
  expire.

#### `DELETE /chat/users/{targetUserId}/block`

**Errors:** `Chat.NotBlocked` (no existing block row from caller → target).

**Response:** `200 OK`, empty body.

---

## 9. Real-Time Communication (SignalR)

### 9.1 Hub route

```
{baseUrl}/hubs/chat
```

Uses the standard ASP.NET Core SignalR protocol (JSON by default). Authentication
is via the `access_token` query parameter — see [Section 2.4](#24-signalr-authentication).

### 9.2 Connecting

Recommended client lifecycle:

1. Log in via REST, obtain `accessToken`.
2. Open the SignalR connection with `access_token` in the query string.
3. On successful connection, call `JoinConversation(conversationId)` for **every**
   conversation currently visible to the user that needs live updates (see
   [Section 9.5](#95-group-membership-is-not-persisted) — this includes the whole
   conversation list, not just the one open on screen, if you want unread-count
   badges to update live in the background).
4. On logout, gracefully stop the connection (there is no explicit server-side
   "logout" hub method — simply disconnect).

### 9.3 Client-invokable hub methods

| Method | Parameters | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| `JoinConversation` | `conversationId: int` | Subscribes the current connection to the SignalR group `chat_conversation_{conversationId}`. Required to receive any conversation-scoped event for that conversation. |
| `LeaveConversation` | `conversationId: int` | Unsubscribes from that group. Does **not** call the REST "leave conversation" endpoint — this is purely a SignalR group membership operation, unrelated to `POST /chat/conversations/{id}/leave`. Naming collision to be aware of. |

There is **no server-side authorization check** on `JoinConversation` — the hub
does not verify the caller is actually a participant of the conversation id
passed in. The frontend must not expose conversation ids from other users'
conversations client-side, since joining a group id is enough to receive its
events.

### 9.4 Server-to-client events

| Event name | Payload shape | Broadcast scope | Fired by |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ReceiveMessage` | Full `ChatMessageDto` (same shape as [Section 8.4](#84-messages) send response) | `chat_conversation_{conversationId}` group | Send message, forward message (once per successfully forwarded target) |
| `MessageEdited` | `{ "messageId": 1042, "newText": "متن ویرایش‌شده", "editedAt": "2026-07-04T06:50:11.123Z" }` | `chat_conversation_{conversationId}` group | Edit message |
| `MessageDeleted` | `{ "messageId": 1042, "conversationId": 55 }` | `chat_conversation_{conversationId}` group | Delete message |
| `MessageRead` | `{ "conversationId": 55, "readByUserId": 12, "lastReadMessageId": 1043 }` | `chat_conversation_{conversationId}` group | Mark-as-read, **`PV` conversations only** |
| `ConversationUpdated` | `{ "conversationId": 55, "unreadCount": 0 }` | `chat_conversation_{conversationId}` group | Mark-as-read, for **any** conversation type (Group/Channel included) |
| `UserStatusChanged` | `{ "userId": 12, "isOnline": true }` | **All connected clients** (`Clients.All` / `Clients.Others`), not scoped to a conversation group | Hub connect/disconnect |
| `SystemMessage` | `{ "conversationId": 61, "text": "سارا احمدی به گروه اضافه شد" }` | `chat_conversation_{conversationId}` group | Member added/removed, name changed, member left |

Event names and payload field casing are exactly as shown (camelCase, since the
default SignalR JSON hub protocol uses the same `System.Text.Json` casing as REST).

### 9.5 Group membership is not persisted

> **Note: differs from originally assumed spec** — SignalR group membership
> (`JoinConversation`) is **connection-scoped**, not user-scoped, and is **not**
> restored automatically by the server on reconnect. Verified against actual
> implementation on Jul 4, 2026.

Practical implication: every time the SignalR connection reconnects (network
blip, app resume from background, token refresh requiring a new connection), the
client must re-call `JoinConversation` for every conversation it cares about, or
it will silently stop receiving events for those conversations with no error. Use
the client library's "reconnected" callback to re-join all groups (see Phase 10
of the implementation plan).

### 9.5.1 `ConversationUpdated` and background badge counts

Because `ConversationUpdatedAsync` broadcasts only to the conversation's own
SignalR group (not to a per-user "my conversations" group), a client that has
**only** joined the conversation currently open on screen will **not** receive
live unread-badge updates for its other conversations. To keep a conversation-list
badge/unread-count UI in sync in real time, the client should join the SignalR
group for **every** conversation in the user's list (not just the open one) —
there is no lighter-weight "subscribe to all my conversations" hub method
available.

### 9.6 Notifications ↔ mute interaction

Sending a message creates a `NotificationCenter` row (this app's general in-app
notification mechanism, also used outside chat) for every other, currently-active,
**non-muted** participant. This is a separate mechanism from the SignalR
`ReceiveMessage` event:

| Recipient state | Gets `ReceiveMessage` (real-time) | Gets `NotificationCenter` entry |
|---|---|---|
| Normal participant | Yes | Yes |
| Muted participant | Yes | **No** |
| Recipient of a forwarded message | Yes | **No** (see [Section 8.4](#84-messages) point 3 and [Section 10](#10-known-backend-limitations)) |

The frontend's own "new message" banner/toast (Phase 9 of the implementation
plan) should be driven by the `ReceiveMessage` event plus the conversation's
client-known `isMuted` flag — do not rely on `NotificationCenter` entries for the
in-chat real-time banner, since that mechanism already excludes muted
conversations and forwarded messages inconsistently with what actually arrives
live.

### 9.7 Online/offline presence

- A user is considered "online" for 2 minutes (sliding) after their most recent
  SignalR connect, refreshed on each new connection; there is no heartbeat/ping
  mechanism beyond the connection itself, and no explicit "still online" refresh
  call — the sliding window is keyed only to connect/disconnect events, so a
  user who stays connected without disconnecting remains "online" indefinitely
  (the sliding timer only matters after a disconnect, to smooth over quick
  reconnects rather than flapping the status).
- `UserStatusChanged` is broadcast **globally** (to every connected client, not
  just people who share a conversation with that user). Any connected client will
  receive presence updates for **all** users, including ones they've never
  chatted with. Filter client-side to the users you actually display presence
  for.
- There is no REST endpoint to fetch a user's **current** online status on demand
  (e.g. when first opening a conversation before any `UserStatusChanged` event has
  fired for that user) — the client's only source of truth is accumulating
  `UserStatusChanged` events after connecting. Assume "unknown/offline" until the
  first event for a given user arrives.

---

## 10. Known Backend Limitations

These are real, verified-in-code behaviors that a QA pass might otherwise flag as
bugs. They are documented here so the frontend team can design around them (and so
QA doesn't waste time re-reporting them). Where a fix requires backend work, that is
noted — do not attempt to work around these on the client beyond what's described.

| # | Limitation | Impact | Frontend guidance |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Timestamp inconsistency**: `sentAt`/`originalSentAt` use the database server's local time (`GETDATE()`), while `editedAt` uses UTC (`DateTime.UtcNow`). | Mixing the two in the same UI (e.g. "sent 2 minutes ago" vs. "edited 2 minutes ago") can show visibly wrong relative times unless handled per-field. | Do not apply a single timezone-conversion function to all message timestamps. Confirm the database server's UTC offset with the backend team before implementing "time ago" formatting; treat `sentAt` as that offset, `editedAt` as UTC. |
| 2 | **Chat profile is not chat-specific.** `PUT /chat/profile` writes directly to the shared `AppUser.Name` / `AppUser.ProfileImage` fields used across the whole app. | Editing your "chat name" changes your name everywhere in the app, not just in chat. | Consider labeling this screen as "Display Name" rather than "Chat Profile" to avoid user confusion, and confirm with the backend/product team whether this is intended before shipping Phase 8. |
| 3 | **PV conversation `name` is empty at creation.** `ConversationListItemDto.name` / `ConversationDetailDto.name` for a `PV` conversation is stored as `""`. | A naive UI would show a blank conversation title/list entry for every PV chat. | The client must derive the display name for PV conversations from the **other participant's** `displayName` (from `ConversationDetailDto.members`, filtering out the caller) rather than trusting the top-level `name` field. This applies to both the list screen and the detail screen. |
| 4 | **Leaving is possible on PV conversations and causes duplicates.** `POST /chat/conversations/{id}/leave` has no server-side guard preventing it from being called on a `PV` conversation. If called, the participant's row gets `leftAt` set. A subsequent `POST /chat/conversations/pv/{targetUserId}` between the same two users will **not** find the old (now-left) conversation (the lookup filters on `leftAt == null`), so a **brand-new duplicate PV conversation** is created, silently breaking the "exactly one PV thread per user pair" rule and orphaning the old conversation's history from being found. | Two duplicate PV threads with the same person; old history becomes unreachable via the normal create-or-get flow (it still exists and is fetchable directly by id, but not discoverable). | Do **not** expose a "leave conversation" action anywhere in the PV chat UI (only in Group/Channel screens). |
| 5 | **Leave system-message wording is group-only.** `LeaveConversationCommandHandler` always sends `"{name} از گروه خارج شد"` ("left the group") regardless of whether the conversation is actually a `Group` or `Channel`. | A member leaving a Channel produces a system message that says "left the group," which is semantically wrong for a channel. | This is a display-text bug in a server-generated string; the frontend cannot correct it without special-casing the exact Persian string, which is fragile. Recommend flagging to the backend team; do not attempt a client-side string patch as a permanent fix. |
| 6 | **Forwarding drops attachments.** `ForwardChatMessageCommandHandler` copies only the original message's `text`; any images/files on the original message are not copied to the forwarded copy. | Forwarding a photo results in a forwarded message with no photo (and, if the original had no caption text, an essentially empty-looking forwarded message). | Consider disabling/hiding the forward action for attachment-only messages, or clearly warn the user in the forward confirmation UI that attachments will not be included, until the backend adds attachment copying. |
| 7 | **Forward has silent partial failure.** Forwarding to multiple target conversations skips (with no error surfaced) any target where the caller isn't a member, or is a non-admin member of a `Channel` target. The HTTP response is `200 OK` regardless of how many (including zero) targets actually succeeded. | The UI cannot show "forwarded successfully to 3 of 4 chats" from the HTTP response alone. | Track `ReceiveMessage` events matching the forwarded content/timing after calling forward, with a short timeout, to approximate delivery confirmation per target. Do not assume success from the `200 OK` alone. |
| 8 | **Forwarded messages don't notify.** Forwarding does not create a `NotificationCenter` entry for recipients (only `SendChatMessage` does), even though it does broadcast `ReceiveMessage` live. | A user who isn't actively viewing the target conversation and relies on the notification center (as opposed to the live in-app banner) will not learn about a forwarded message. | Do not treat this as a bug during QA — it is a verified, current backend behavior. If your Phase 9 in-app banner is driven off `ReceiveMessage` (as recommended in Section 9.6), forwarded messages will still show a live banner if the app is open; only the persistent notification-center list will miss them. |
| 9 | **No promote/demote/transfer-ownership.** There is no endpoint anywhere in the Chat API to change a Group or Channel member's role after creation. The creator is made `Admin` at creation time; every other initial or later-added member is always `Member`. Admins can only be removed from a Group/Channel by leaving voluntarily — no one can demote them, and no one can promote a `Member` to `Admin`. | If the sole admin leaves a Group/Channel, that conversation permanently has **zero** admins — no one can ever again add/remove members, edit its info, or (for Channels) send messages. | Do not build any promote/demote/transfer-ownership UI (see Phase 6/7 of the implementation plan). Strongly consider a confirmation warning when the *only* admin attempts to leave a Group/Channel ("You are the only admin — leaving will make this group unmanageable"), computed client-side from the member list's role distribution, since the backend enforces no such warning itself. |
| 10 | **No self-service channel join by handle.** Despite channels having a unique `handle`, there is no endpoint to look up a channel by handle or to self-join a channel. The only way to become a channel member is to be added by an existing admin via `POST /chat/channels/{id}/members`. | A "join by handle/link" discovery flow cannot be built against this backend today. | Do not build a join-by-handle screen (see Phase 7). The `handle` field is currently write-once metadata with no corresponding read/join API. |
| 11 | **`JoinConversation` has no authorization check.** Any authenticated SignalR connection can join any conversation's group by id and receive its events, without a server-side membership check. | A malicious or buggy client could receive events for conversations it isn't a member of, if it can guess/enumerate conversation ids. | Never expose or accept conversation ids from an untrusted source in the client; only join ids returned by the authenticated REST endpoints for the current user's own conversation list/detail calls. |
| 12 | **No online-status query endpoint.** There is no REST way to ask "is user X online right now" — only the cumulative stream of `UserStatusChanged` events after connecting. | A freshly opened PV chat has no presence indicator until the other user's next connect/disconnect event happens to fire. | Default to an "unknown" (or hidden) presence indicator until the first event for that user is observed in the current session. |
| 13 | **Deleted messages don't hide their attachments.** `DELETE /chat/messages/{id}` nulls `text` but leaves `attachments` fully populated in subsequent `GET` responses. | A "deleted" message's files remain visible/downloadable unless the client hides them itself. | When `isDeleted == true`, the client should suppress rendering of `attachments` even though the API still returns them, to match user expectations of what "delete" means. |

---

*End of document. See `CHAT_FRONTEND_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` for the phased Flutter
build plan that references the sections above.*

input.contents:
# Chat Module — Frontend Implementation Plan (Flutter)

This document is a phased build plan for implementing the Elay Customer Chat
module in the Flutter app. Each phase is scoped to be independently buildable and
testable before starting the next. All endpoint and event references point to
sections in `CHAT_API_DOCUMENTATION.md`, which is the authoritative source for
request/response shapes, validation rules, and error codes — this plan does not
repeat that detail, only how and when to use it.

Read `CHAT_API_DOCUMENTATION.md` Section 10 ("Known Backend Limitations") before
starting Phase 1 — several phases below explicitly call out UI that must **not**
be built because of those limitations.

---

## Table of Contents

- [Recommended Flutter Packages](#recommended-flutter-packages)
- [Phase 1 — Foundation](#phase-1--foundation)
- [Phase 2 — Home & Discovery](#phase-2--home--discovery)
- [Phase 3 — PV Core Messaging](#phase-3--pv-core-messaging)
- [Phase 4 — Rich Messaging](#phase-4--rich-messaging)
- [Phase 5 — PV Relationship Management](#phase-5--pv-relationship-management)
- [Phase 6 — Groups](#phase-6--groups)
- [Phase 7 — Channels](#phase-7--channels)
- [Phase 8 — Profile](#phase-8--profile)
- [Phase 9 — Notifications](#phase-9--notifications)
- [Phase 10 — Resilience & Polish](#phase-10--resilience--polish)

---

## Recommended Flutter Packages

| Concern | Package | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| HTTP client | `dio` | Interceptor support for attaching the JWT and handling 401/429 centrally. |
| Real-time (SignalR) | `signalr_netcore` | Actively maintained SignalR client compatible with ASP.NET Core SignalR's JSON hub protocol; supports query-string auth and automatic reconnect callbacks needed for Section 9.5's re-join logic. |
| Secure token storage | `flutter_secure_storage` | Store access + refresh tokens in Keychain/Keystore rather than `SharedPreferences`. |
| State management | `flutter_bloc` or `riverpod` (pick one, be consistent) | Needed to fan out SignalR events (which arrive outside the normal request/response cycle) to multiple screens (conversation list badge, open chat screen, notification banner) simultaneously. |
| File picking | `file_picker` (documents), `image_picker` (avatars/photos) | Both needed — `image_picker` alone doesn't handle arbitrary attachment types. |
| Image caching/display | `cached_network_image` | Avatars and attachment thumbnails are fetched repeatedly across screens; avoid re-downloading. |
| Connectivity detection | `connectivity_plus` | Drives the offline banner in Phase 10. |
| Local notifications (optional, if in-app banner isn't sufficient) | `flutter_local_notifications` | Only needed if product wants OS-level notifications while the app is foregrounded; the in-app banner in Phase 9 does not require this package. |
| Date/time formatting with locale | `intl` | For Persian/Jalali-aware relative time formatting ("چند دقیقه پیش"). |
| Debouncing search input | `easy_debounce` (or a hand-rolled `Timer`) | For the search boxes in Phases 2 and 6/7 member pickers. |

---

## Phase 1 — Foundation

**Goal:** A working authenticated HTTP + SignalR pipeline with no chat UI yet.
This phase is a prerequisite for every later phase.

**Backend references:** API Docs §2 (Authentication), §6 (Rate Limiting), §9.1–9.2
(SignalR connection).

**Screens/components:**

- `LoginScreen` (username/password form, calls `POST /Auth/Login`).
- `ApiClient` (Dio instance) with:
  - Request interceptor: attaches `Authorization: Bearer {accessToken}` from
    secure storage to every request.
  - Response interceptor: on `401`, attempts `POST /Auth/RefreshToken` once and
    retries the original request; on repeated failure, forces logout back to
    `LoginScreen`.
  - Response interceptor: on `429`, surfaces a typed exception the caller can
    react to (full backoff/retry UX lands in Phase 10, but the plumbing to detect
    it belongs here).
- `SecureTokenStorage` wrapper around `flutter_secure_storage` (get/set/clear
  access + refresh tokens).
- `ChatSocketService` wrapping `signalr_netcore`:
  - `connect()` — builds the hub URL with `?access_token=...` (API Docs §2.4) and
    starts the connection.
  - `disconnect()` — called on logout.
  - Exposes typed streams/callbacks for each event in API Docs §9.4
    (`ReceiveMessage`, `MessageEdited`, `MessageDeleted`, `MessageRead`,
    `ConversationUpdated`, `UserStatusChanged`, `SystemMessage`) so later phases
    don't need to touch raw SignalR registration code.
  - `joinConversation(int id)` / `leaveConversation(int id)` wrappers around the
    two hub methods (API Docs §9.3).
  - A reconnect callback hook (`onReconnected`) that later phases (Phase 10) will
    use to re-join all previously-joined conversation groups — stub it now so the
    interface exists, even if the re-join list is empty until Phase 2 exists.

**Connection lifecycle rules to implement now:**

- Connect the SignalR client **immediately after** a successful login (or on cold
  app start if a valid stored token exists).
- Disconnect it **before** clearing stored tokens on logout (order matters —
  disconnecting after clearing the token means the disconnect call itself may
  fail auth, though this is a minor edge case).
- Do not attempt to connect SignalR before the REST login call has returned a
  token — there is no anonymous/guest chat connection.

**Definition of done:**

- [ ] Can log in and see a stored access + refresh token in secure storage.
- [ ] Every subsequent REST call automatically carries the bearer token with no
      manual wiring per-call-site.
- [ ] An expired/invalid access token triggers an automatic refresh-and-retry
      exactly once, then logs the user out if that also fails.
- [ ] SignalR connects successfully right after login (verify via a debug log or
      breakpoint on the hub's `OnConnectedAsync`, or by checking a
      `UserStatusChanged` event fires for this user on another test device).
- [ ] SignalR disconnects cleanly on logout.
- [ ] `joinConversation` / `leaveConversation` wrapper methods exist and compile,
      even though nothing calls them yet.

## Phase 2 — Home & Discovery

**Goal:** A conversation list that loads, searches, and reorders in real time,
plus the ability to start a new PV chat from a user list.

**Backend references:** API Docs §8.2 (`GET /chat/users`), §8.3
(`GET /chat/conversations`, `POST /chat/conversations/pv/{targetUserId}`),
§9.4 (`ReceiveMessage`), §10 limitation #3 (PV conversation name is empty).

**Screens/components:**

- `ConversationListScreen`:
  - Fetches `GET /chat/conversations` on load.
  - For **every** conversation returned, calls `joinConversation(id)` (from
    Phase 1) so the badge/reorder logic below has live data — see API Docs
    §9.5.1 on why joining only the open chat isn't enough for background badges.
  - Renders each item's `name` — for `type == "PV"`, the raw `name` field will be
    empty (Known Limitation #3); this screen must instead resolve the other
    participant's display name. The list endpoint doesn't return member info
    directly, so either (a) fetch `GET /chat/conversations/{id}` lazily per PV
    row and cache the resolved name, or (b) derive it once when the conversation
    is first opened/created and cache it locally — pick one strategy and apply it
    consistently; do not leave PV rows blank.
  - Subscribes to `ReceiveMessage`: on any event, update that conversation's
    `lastMessage`/`lastMessageAt` in local state and re-sort the list
    (`lastMessageAt` descending), without a full re-fetch.
  - Subscribes to `ConversationUpdated`: update that conversation's
    `unreadCount` badge in place.
  - Empty state: "شما هنوز مکالمه‌ای ندارید" (no conversations yet) when the list
    is empty and no search is active.
  - Search box (debounced) calls `GET /chat/conversations?search=...`; empty
    state for zero results: "نتیجه‌ای یافت نشد" (no search results), distinct
    from the "no conversations at all" state.
- `NewChatUserPickerScreen`:
  - Fetches `GET /chat/users?search=...` (debounced search-as-you-type).
  - Tapping a user calls `POST /chat/conversations/pv/{targetUserId}` (safe to
    call even if a PV already exists — it's create-or-get) and navigates into
    the resulting conversation (Phase 3).
  - Empty state for zero search results, same pattern as above.

**Definition of done:**

- [ ] Conversation list loads and displays correctly for all three conversation
      types, with PV rows showing the other participant's name, not a blank
      title.
- [ ] Sending a message from another device (or a second test session) visibly
      reorders this screen's list to the top in real time, without a manual pull-
      to-refresh.
- [ ] Unread badges update live via `ConversationUpdated` without a full list
      refetch.
- [ ] Both empty states (no conversations vs. no search results) are visually
      distinct and correctly triggered.
- [ ] Starting a new PV chat with a user who already has an existing PV
      conversation opens the *existing* thread (verify no duplicate is created).

## Phase 3 — PV Core Messaging

**Goal:** Fully working one-to-one text messaging: open a PV chat, see history,
send/receive live, scroll-load older messages, and see delivery/read state.

**Backend references:** API Docs §7 (Pagination), §8.4
(`GET`/`POST /chat/conversations/{id}/messages`), §8.3
(`POST /chat/conversations/{id}/read`), §9.4 (`ReceiveMessage`, `MessageRead`),
§4 (timestamp caveats).

**Screens/components:**

- `ChatScreen` (reusable — this phase builds it for PV; Phase 6 reuses it for
  Group with minimal changes):
  - On open: call `joinConversation(conversationId)` (idempotent — safe even if
    already joined from Phase 2's list screen); fetch the first page via
    `GET /chat/conversations/{id}/messages` (no `beforeMessageId`).
  - Infinite-scroll-up pagination: when the user scrolls near the top and
    `hasMore == true`, fetch the next page using `oldestMessageId` as
    `beforeMessageId` and prepend results.
  - Sending: `POST /chat/conversations/{id}/messages` with `text` only for this
    phase (attachments/reply/edit/delete/forward arrive in Phase 4). Optimistically
    render the outgoing message with a "sending" tick immediately, then reconcile
    with the server response (real `id`, `sentAt`) when it returns.
  - Subscribe to `ReceiveMessage` scoped to this conversation id; append incoming
    messages live. De-duplicate against the optimistic local echo of the sender's
    own sent messages (match by a client-generated temp id or by content+timing
    if no temp-id correlation is implemented).
  - Call `POST /chat/conversations/{id}/read` with the newest visible message id
    whenever the user views the bottom of the chat (on open, and again whenever
    new messages arrive while the screen is foregrounded). Subscribe to
    `MessageRead` to update the sent-message ticks to "read" when the other
    participant reads.
  - Message status ticks: "sent" (HTTP response received) → "delivered" (cannot
    be distinguished from "sent" by this backend today — there is no separate
    delivery acknowledgment event, only `MessageRead`; treat "sent" and
    "delivered" as the same visual state) → "read" (via `MessageRead` for PV
    conversations only, per API Docs §9.4).
  - Send-failure retry UI: if the `POST` call fails (network error, validation
    error, 429), keep the optimistic bubble but mark it with a visible error/retry
    affordance; tapping retry re-submits the same content.
  - Timestamps: render `sentAt` per the guidance in API Docs §4 — do not apply a
    UTC-to-local conversion to it.

**Definition of done:**

- [ ] Opening a PV chat loads the most recent 20 messages.
- [ ] Scrolling to the top loads older pages seamlessly, with no duplicate or
      skipped messages at page boundaries.
- [ ] A message sent from this device appears instantly (optimistic) and is not
      duplicated when the corresponding `ReceiveMessage` event arrives.
- [ ] A message sent from a second test session/device appears live without a
      manual refresh.
- [ ] Read ticks update in real time when the other participant opens the chat.
- [ ] A forced network failure while sending shows a retry affordance, and
      retrying successfully sends the message.
- [ ] No timestamp is off by the server's UTC offset (spot-check against a known
      wall-clock send time).

## Phase 4 — Rich Messaging

**Goal:** Attachments, replies, edit, delete, and forward — all layered on top of
the Phase 3 `ChatScreen`.

**Backend references:** API Docs §8.4 (send with `attachments`, edit, delete,
forward), §8.5 (standalone upload — read the note on when it's actually needed),
§5 (file URL construction), §10 limitations #6, #7, #8, #13.

**Screens/components:**

- **Attachments:**
  - Compose bar gets an attach button → `file_picker`/`image_picker` → client-
    side size check against 50 MB (52,428,800 bytes) **before** upload, showing
    an immediate error if exceeded rather than waiting for the server's
    `Chat.FileTooLarge` response (still handle that server error too, as a
    fallback for edge cases like a file changing size between pick and send).
  - Selected files are sent as part of the same
    `POST /chat/conversations/{id}/messages` multipart call as `text` — do
    **not** call the standalone `POST /chat/files` endpoint for the normal
    "attach and send" flow (see API Docs §8.5's note on this).
  - Render received attachments using `ChatFileDto.fileUrl` prefixed with
    `{baseUrl}/chat_files/` (API Docs §5) — never the `/images/` prefix used for
    avatars.
  - Image attachments: inline thumbnail + full-screen viewer. Non-image
    attachments: filename + size + a download/open action.
- **Reply-to:**
  - Swipe/long-press a message → "reply" → shows a quoted preview bar above the
    compose box (`replyTo.textPreview`, `replyTo.senderName`) → included as
    `replyToMessageId` on send.
  - Rendering a message with a non-null `replyTo`: show a compact quote block
    above the message bubble; if `replyTo.isDeleted == true`, show a
    "پیام اصلی حذف شده است" placeholder instead of the (nulled) preview text.
- **Edit:**
  - Only show the edit action on the current user's own messages
    (`senderId == myUserId`) — the backend rejects edits from anyone else
    anyway (`Chat.CannotEditMessage`), but hiding it avoids a round-trip error.
  - On save, call `PUT /chat/messages/{id}`; on the `MessageEdited` event (own or
    others'), update the message's `text` and show an "edited" label, using the
    event's `editedAt` (UTC — convert to local for display, per API Docs §4).
- **Delete:**
  - Show the delete action for the sender always, and additionally for the
    conversation's admin (for Group/Channel — determine admin-ness from the
    conversation detail's `currentUserRole`).
  - On `DELETE /chat/messages/{id}` success or on receiving `MessageDeleted` for
    any message, replace that message's rendered content with a client-owned
    placeholder (e.g. "این پیام حذف شده است") — the backend does not supply this
    text (Known Limitation). Per Known Limitation #13, also hide any attachments
    on a message once `isDeleted` is true, even though the API still returns
    them.
- **Forward:**
  - Long-press → "forward" → multi-select target picker listing the user's own
    conversations (reuse the Phase 2 conversation list data, minus the source
    conversation itself).
  - Call `POST /chat/messages/{id}/forward` with the selected
    `targetConversationIds`.
  - Because the backend silently skips invalid targets and never reports which
    targets succeeded (Known Limitation #7), show a generic
    "در حال ارسال..." state after submitting, then rely on incoming
    `ReceiveMessage` events (matching the forwarded content) within a short
    window (e.g. 3–5 seconds) to confirm-per-target in the picker UI; after the
    window, mark any still-unconfirmed targets as "ممکن است ارسال نشده باشد"
    (may not have been delivered) rather than claiming guaranteed success.
  - Do not offer forwarding of attachment-only messages without a warning that
    the attachment will be dropped (Known Limitation #6) — show a one-time
    inline notice in the forward sheet when the source message has attachments
    and no text.

**Definition of done:**

- [ ] Selecting a file over 50 MB is rejected client-side before any network
      call, with a clear Persian-friendly message.
- [ ] Sending an image and a non-image file both render correctly for the
      recipient in real time.
- [ ] Replying shows the quoted context correctly, including the deleted-original
      placeholder case.
- [ ] Editing a message updates it live for all participants and shows the
      "edited" indicator.
- [ ] Deleting a message replaces it with a client-rendered placeholder for all
      participants, with attachments hidden.
- [ ] Forwarding a text message to multiple targets shows a reasonable
      best-effort delivery confirmation, and forwarding an attachment-only
      message warns the user the attachment will not carry over.

## Phase 5 — PV Relationship Management

**Goal:** Block/unblock and mute/unmute, correctly reflecting the backend's
"read-only after block" model.

**Backend references:** API Docs §8.8 (block/unblock), §8.3 (mute),
§8.4 (`Chat.UserBlocked` error on send).

**Screens/components:**

- Block/unblock action (e.g. in a PV chat's overflow menu or the other user's
  profile sheet):
  - `POST /chat/users/{targetUserId}/block` / `DELETE /chat/users/{targetUserId}/block`.
  - **Critical UX requirement:** per verified backend behavior, blocking does
    **not** hide the conversation or its history — it only rejects new outgoing
    messages (in either direction, since the check is bidirectional). Do **not**
    hide the conversation from the list or clear its history on block. Instead:
    - Keep the conversation visible and its history scrollable/readable as
      normal.
    - Disable the message compose box (text input + attach + send button) when
      the client knows a block relationship is active in either direction.
    - Show a persistent, clearly worded banner above the disabled compose box,
      e.g. "شما این کاربر را بلاک کرده‌اید" (you blocked this user) or
      "امکان ارسال پیام به این کاربر وجود ندارد" (matching the server's own
      `Chat.UserBlocked` message) — **not** a transient toast, since the
      condition is persistent, not a one-off error.
  - Since there is no `GET` endpoint that reports current block state directly
    (it's not part of `ConversationDetailDto`), the client must track its own
    block/unblock actions locally (e.g. persisted per-user flag) to know when to
    show the disabled-compose banner proactively, in addition to reactively
    handling a `Chat.UserBlocked` error if the user still manages to attempt a
    send (e.g. from a stale UI state after being blocked *by* the other party,
    which the client has no way to detect proactively since blocking is silent
    per Known Limitation — see API Docs §8.8).
  - Because block-by-the-other-party is silent and undetectable ahead of time,
    always handle the `Chat.UserBlocked` error from the send-message call
    gracefully regardless of the client's own locally tracked block state —
    show the same persistent banner reactively the moment that error is
    received, rather than a one-off toast.
- Mute/unmute toggle (conversation-level, works for PV/Group/Channel alike):
  - `POST /chat/conversations/{id}/mute` with `{ "isMuted": true|false }`.
  - Reflect `isMuted` from `ConversationListItemDto`/`ConversationDetailDto` as a
    persistent icon/state (e.g. a muted-bell icon in the conversation list row
    and inside the chat screen's header).
  - Per API Docs §9.6, muting does not stop live `ReceiveMessage` delivery — only
    suppresses the notification-center entry. Ensure the Phase 9 notification
    banner logic checks the local `isMuted` flag before showing a banner, since
    the backend will still push the event.

**Definition of done:**

- [ ] Blocking a user disables that PV chat's compose box immediately, with the
      persistent banner shown, while history remains fully visible and
      scrollable.
- [ ] Unblocking re-enables the compose box.
- [ ] Attempting to send while blocked (either via a stale UI or a block that
      happened from the other side) shows the same persistent banner, not a
      one-off error toast, upon receiving `Chat.UserBlocked`.
- [ ] Muting a conversation persists across app restarts (reflected from the
      server's `isMuted` field, not just local state) and suppresses the Phase 9
      banner for that conversation while still delivering messages live into the
      open chat screen if the user is actively viewing it.
